title: helianthus diffusus var. missouriensis
intro: Helianthus diffusus var. missouriensis, also known as Missouri sunflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the prairies of the Midwestern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Missouri sunflower has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antitussive properties, offering relief from coughs and congestion. Herbal preparations of Helianthus diffusus var. missouriensis, such as teas and infusions, have been used to promote overall health and well-being, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system function.
